# @verevoir/access

> Identity resolution, policy evaluation, and workflow state machines. Zero runtime dependencies. Standalone — no Verevoir package imports required.

## Install

```bash
npm install @verevoir/access
```

## Core Exports

### Identity & Auth

```typescript
import { defineAuthAdapter, ANONYMOUS, isAnonymous } from '@verevoir/access';

// Define how tokens resolve to identities
const auth = defineAuthAdapter({
  resolve: async (token: string): Promise<Identity | null> => {
    // Verify token, return identity
    return { id: 'user-123', roles: ['admin'], metadata: { name: 'Alice' } };
  },
});

const identity = await auth.resolve(token);

// ANONYMOUS — frozen identity with roles: ['viewer']
// isAnonymous(identity) — structural check (not reference equality)
```

### Policy & Authorisation

```typescript
import { definePolicy, can, hasRole, isOwner, and, or, not } from '@verevoir/access';

const policy = definePolicy({
  rules: [
    { action: 'read', guard: hasRole('viewer') },
    { action: 'create', guard: hasRole('editor') },
    { action: 'update', guard: or(hasRole('admin'), and(hasRole('editor'), isOwner())) },
    { action: 'delete', guard: hasRole('admin') },
  ],
});

// Check permission
const allowed = can(policy, identity, 'update', { ownerId: identity.id });
```

Guards: `hasRole(role)`, `isOwner()`, `and(...guards)`, `or(...guards)`, `not(guard)`

The `isOwner()` guard checks `identity.id === context.ownerId`.

### Workflow

Stateless state machine with guard-protected transitions.

```typescript
import { defineWorkflow, hasRole } from '@verevoir/access';

const publishing = defineWorkflow({
  states: ['draft', 'review', 'published', 'archived'],
  transitions: [
    { from: 'draft', to: 'review', guard: hasRole('editor') },
    { from: 'review', to: 'published', guard: hasRole('admin') },
    { from: 'review', to: 'draft', guard: hasRole('editor') },
    { from: 'published', to: 'archived', guard: hasRole('admin') },
  ],
});

// Get available transitions for current state and identity
const transitions = publishing.transitions('draft', identity);
// [{ from: 'draft', to: 'review', guard: ... }] if identity has 'editor' role
```

## Subpath Exports

### @verevoir/access/google

Google OAuth2 auth adapter. Optional peer dep: `google-auth-library`.

```typescript
import { createGoogleAuthAdapter } from '@verevoir/access/google';

const auth = createGoogleAuthAdapter({
  clientId: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
  mapRoles: async (googleId) => ['viewer'], // sync or async
});
```

### @verevoir/access/test-accounts

Lookup-table auth adapter for development and testing.

```typescript
import { createTestAuthAdapter } from '@verevoir/access/test-accounts';

const auth = createTestAuthAdapter([
  { token: 'admin-token', identity: { id: 'admin-1', roles: ['admin'] } },
  { token: 'viewer-token', identity: { id: 'viewer-1', roles: ['viewer'] } },
]);
```

### @verevoir/access/role-store

Persistent user-to-roles mapping via StorageAdapter (structural type — no import from @verevoir/storage).

```typescript
import { createRoleStore } from '@verevoir/access/role-store';

const roleStore = createRoleStore({
  storage: db, // any StorageAdapter-compatible object
  seedAdmin: 'google-123', // optional: auto-creates admin on first match
});

const roles = await roleStore.getRoles('google-123');
await roleStore.setRoles('google-456', ['editor']);
const all = await roleStore.listAssignments();
```

## Types

```typescript
interface Identity {
  id: string;
  roles: string[];
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
}

interface RoleStore {
  getRoles(userId: string): Promise<string[]>;
  setRoles(userId: string, roles: string[]): Promise<void>;
  listAssignments(): Promise<{ userId: string; roles: string[] }[]>;
}
```
